question.text: Which of the following must be disclosed in the PPM regarding the management team of an AIF as per SEBI requirements?

answer.text: Only the name of the Chief Investment Officer
Personal financial statements of all team members
Background, experience, and track record of key investment team members, including key persons identified under the Key Person Clause
The educational qualifications of the administrative staff

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI requires the PPM to disclose the background, qualifications, experience, and track record of key investment professionals, especially those designated under the Key Person Clause. This enables investors to assess management capability. Personal financials and admin staff details are not required, and disclosing only the CIO is insufficient. **The Trap:** Disclosing only the CIO name is partially correct but incomplete — SEBI requires disclosure of all key persons, not just the CIO. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) requires similar disclosure of analyst credentials to ensure transparency. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The management team section is among the most important in the PPM. A strong, stable team with a proven track record is often a better predictor of performance than the stated strategy.
